hypothesis: Establish baseline at Level 3 promotion seed count (5 seeds)
lessons: Baseline tail-class NLL is the obvious weakness.

hypothesis: Aggressive ordinal hybrid loss with coefficient=0.9 should improve NLL further than the 0.5 default
failure_reason: preflight_fail_fast: Stage-A tiny-overfit test loss diverged at step 12 (NaN gradient at coefficient=0.9). Per §17.1.1 preflight, full Stage-C budget NOT consumed.
lessons: Ordinal hybrid coefficient is sensitive at the upper end; tiny-overfit catches divergence before consuming the budget.
Future ordinal-hybrid sweeps should grid-search coefficient between 0.1 and 0.7, NOT extend to 0.9.
next_actions: Carry forward as known_failures: coefficient ≥ 0.9 is unstable.

hypothesis: Cosine restarts schedule should improve NLL by re-warming the optimizer past local minima
failure_reason: guardrail_regression: inference_latency_ms Δ 95% CI upper = +2.1ms, exceeds Holm-corrected threshold +1.84ms (10% relative to baseline 18.4ms). Per §13.2.1, GUARDRAIL-FAIL; per §13.4, candidate cannot promote.
lessons: Cosine restarts on this task increase latency variance; some checkpoints land in slower kernels due to attention activation patterns differing per restart phase.
Optimization branch is not a free lunch when guardrails include latency.

hypothesis: Attention pooling should improve NLL within the latency budget
lessons: Attention pool is the strongest architecture branch on this task.
Subject to confirmation by an ablation (next iteration).

hypothesis: N/A — this is an §17.1.1 behavioral-equivalence failure that invalidates prior runs
invalidation_reason: behavioral_equivalence_test_failed: a clarity refactor of evaluation/metric_defs.py changed the rounding mode from 'round-half-even' to 'round-half-up'. Behavioral-equivalence test caught the drift on the next preflight; per §17.1.1 the human review judged the change semantic (not byte-equivalent), updated the golden fixtures (archived old goldens under evaluation/fixtures/archive/20260518/), and retroactively invalidated iter-3's metrics pending re-grade. Iter-3 is NOT a failed result; the evaluator change invalidates the comparison.
lessons: Evaluator refactors require behavioral-equivalence check BEFORE merge to the protected branch.
Golden-fixture updates are a quarterly-frequency event; we just did one — track for §17.1.1 'multiple updates per quarter' warning.
next_actions: Re-grade iter-3 candidate under updated evaluator (iter-5)

hypothesis: After evaluator refresh, does iter-3's attention pool improvement persist?
lessons: Iter-3 effect persists under the updated evaluator (NLL Δ = -0.022 vs the prior -0.024; within seed variance band).
Re-grades after evaluator refresh are double-charged to val exposure — cost-tier seed counts should be revisited mid-campaign if multiple refreshes happen.

hypothesis: Combining attention pool with ordinal hybrid loss should produce additive gains
failure_reason: protocol_violation_caught_by_skeptic: Proposal flips TWO non-baseline switches simultaneously. Per §11.1.1 this is a stack, not a candidate. Per §16.1.2, promotion requires factorial ablation grid before a stack can be considered. Skeptic returned proposal to Research Director for factorial planning; no experiment was run.
lessons: §11.1.1 enforcement: agent must propose single-switch candidates OR explicitly request factorial planning.
Effective Skeptic review depends on Level-2 separation; same-session Level-0 would have likely missed this.

hypothesis: Per §16.1.2 factorial: does ordinal_ce_hybrid contribute beyond attention_pool?
lessons: Attention pool drives 76% of the combined gain; ordinal_hybrid contributes a small, just-significant 24%; no interaction.
Decision: promote attention pool alone. Carry ordinal_hybrid as a 'promising' branch for future campaigns.
Cost was 36.4 GPU-hours — twice a typical candidate. Factorials are expensive; consider whether the marginal information was worth it.
next_actions: Plan §16.1.1 lesion test on attention_pool (attention weights → uniform)
Emit promotion request for attention_pool alone

hypothesis: Promote attention_pool based on iter-5 + iter-7 evidence
rejection_reason: verifier_rejected_promotion_request: Per §10.5 rule 6 (val exposure not exhausted): queries=52 ≥ budget=50. Cumulative val exposure passed the configured budget during the iter-7 factorial (which added 12 queries) and an additional 2 seeds for the §16.1.1 lesion test ablation. Verifier signed packet with status: rejected. To proceed, a holdout refresh (§17.6.3) is required, which is a §3.1 escalation requiring human review.

artifacts.lesion_test_results: validation_nll with attention_weights replaced by uniform = 0.846 (within seed variance of baseline); confirms attention mechanism (not surrounding scaffolding) drives the gain

lessons: The §17.6 budget was set too tight for a campaign that included a factorial. Plan campaigns by counting expected total queries up front: baseline (5) + candidates (3 each) + reruns + factorials × cells × seeds + ablations × seeds.
An honest 'rejected' from the verifier is the protocol working as intended. The agent does not get to override its own promotion.
next_actions: Request holdout refresh from human reviewer per §17.6.3
If refresh approved, re-emit promotion_request with reset exposure counter
Document the budget-planning lesson in playbook
